prior.content_md:
**MONITORING** — Review European road carrier agreements for Euro 7 surcharge or fleet-renewal pass-through clauses before the heavy-duty enforcement date of 1 May 2028.

*Action:* Instruct commercial and operations leads to audit current road carrier contracts on European lanes for any clause permitting unilateral surcharge imposition linked to regulatory compliance costs. Identify contracts renewable before May 2027 as priority for renegotiation windows.
*Cost impact:* Carrier-level cost pass-through quantum is not publicly available as a sourced figure at this date. Directional pressure is upward: fleet renewal costs for Euro 7-compliant heavy-duty vehicles will be higher than Euro 6 equivalents, particularly for vehicles requiring new brake particle filtration and tyre abrasion mitigation systems. undefined undefined Cost effect on specific verticals requires carrier-specific data.
*Who is affected:* Operations leads managing European road carrier relationships; commercial leads quoting road-mode rates on European lanes for live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian cargo.
*Why now:* The May 2028 enforcement date for heavy-duty vehicles is 25 months from the date of this brief. Carrier investment cycles for fleet renewal typically run 18–36 months. Carriers are making procurement decisions now that will determine their surcharge posture by 2027–2028.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2024/1257, Article 4 application dates. Tier 1 — binding EU law.*

**MONITORING** — COST ALERT flag contingent on carrier surcharge activity. Monitor freight market reporting for announced Euro 7 surcharges from European road carriers, particularly specialist and time-critical operators serving the workspace's verticals.

*Action:* Establish a monitoring trigger: if any road carrier on a European lane issues written notice of a Euro 7-linked surcharge before May 2027, escalate to COST ALERT and initiate commercial review.
*Who is affected:* Commercial leadership responsible for European road-mode rate structures.
*Why now:* No sourced carrier surcharge announcements have been identified as of the date of this brief. This absence does not mean the cost pressure is absent — it means the signal has not yet reached the market reporting layer. The monitoring posture is appropriate now; the escalation trigger is the first surcharge announcement.

---
